title: Allow permissions to carry over from Templates
excerpt:
Currently, when one creates a board from a template, the permissions on that board do not carry over. For example, permissions who can view a specific column. 
Please can we submit a feature request to allow permissions …

posts.author: danid (Dani Drutman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Currently, when one creates a board from a template, the permissions on that board do not carry over. For example, permissions who can view a specific column.

Please can we submit a feature request to allow permissions to carry over?

posts.author: TerryE (Terry Evans)
posts.cookedHtml: I can not imagine, for the life of me, why… You can assign permissions on column and subitem columns within a template, and those permissions do not carry over to a board created from that template.

posts.author: rcalkins (Russell Calkins)
posts.cookedHtml:
We would like to use a bunch of columns in to wire together different boards, and then stamp them out using templates.

The problem is none of the column permissions survive the templating process. It deletes all the permissions except the new board owner, requiring somebody to manually rewire every single board that gets made.

I would like column permissions set in the template to carry through to boards that get made.

Particularly teams. This would help immensely. As I could tag our Ops team for a bunch of column wiring, and then hide it from the rest of the organization.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml: Wouldn’t it be nice to setup board permissions in your private templates? Or save a current board with board permissions as a template. In my use case (I use 4 different boards for 1 solution) I need to set board permissions each time I create a solution (need to set it in 4 boards).

posts.author: JustinJ (Justin Junioous)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @basdebruin,

Justin here Thank you for sharing this feedback. I agree this would be useful to have.

I can not guarantee anything, but I will be sure to pass this along to our team for review. Keep rocking it and sharing your thoughts.

You help make us better. Feel free to share any use cases you may have in other areas as well.

Cheers,
Justin

posts.author: bhowell (Bryan Howell)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m confused as to why I am unable to set the board permissions on a templated board… The option is grayed out in the menu. See below:

Screen Shot 2020-03-23 at 9.03.16 AM3152×376 79.9 KB

posts.author: dmalone (Duane)
posts.cookedHtml:
Template board permission settings are still not being passed on to their children. This would be really great to have!

Thanks.

posts.author: Scott1 (Scott Shields)
posts.cookedHtml:
Can a feature be added to maintain the Column Permissions set at the Template level?

Currently, the column permissions do not carry over when creating a new project using a custom template. The permissions need to be re-added for every new project

Screenshot 2024-07-15 at 1.26.15 PM692×344 23.7 KB

posts.author: maalfunction (Beatriz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Checking on this feature.
The settings on the Template board should be carried through, including all visibility permissions and board ownership…

posts.author: Bridget1 (Bridget McDonald)
posts.cookedHtml: Whilst I hate having to use a work around, I’ve found that if you create an automation it will work with board owners, I haven’t tried with permissions. I have “when an item is moved (could be almost anything) and status is … then create a board from template”. Under board, go to advanced and choose board owners. I’ve found by doing that, I only have to select the board owners that one time.
